'''Jordan Abdull''' (born 5 February 1996) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has played for [[Hull FC]] and [[Hull KR]] in the [[Super League]], [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and on loan at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]],<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Statistics at donsstats.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.donsstats.co.uk/player.php?PID=1127|title=Statistics at donsstats.co.uk|publisher=donsstats.co.uk|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}}</ref> [[Featherstone Rovers]] and [[Hull KR]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Abdull has also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the top flight and played as a {{rlp|lf}} earlier in his career.

==Background==
Abdull was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], East Yorkshire.

== Early life ==
Abdull began his rugby league career at amateur club the Norland Sharks A.R.L.F.C. (in [[Hessle]], East Riding of Yorkshire, of the [[Yorkshire Men's League]]),<ref name="Norland Sharks ARLFC at pitchero.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.pitchero.com/clubs/norlandsharksarlfc|title=Norland Sharks ARLFC at pitchero.com|publisher=pitchero.com|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}}</ref> before moving to Skirlaugh Bulls ARLFC, and at school level he reached three Carnegie [[Champion Schools]] National Cup finals whilst at [[Sirius Academy West|Sirius Academy]].

== Career ==
===Hull FC===
After signing for boyhood club [[Hull F.C.]], he made his first team début against the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on 29 June 2014, in which he kicked his first career conversion. Later that season he signed a new two-year contract with the club.<ref>{{cite news|title=Jordan Abdull: Stand-off signs new two-year Hull FC deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28900527|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 September 2014|date=22 August 2014}}</ref>
In the 2014 season, Abdull scored his first try for Hull F.C. against the [[Castleford Tigers]] in an 18–18 draw on 24 July. His form saw him gain a call-up to the England Academy side on 19 October 2014, alongside Hull U19s teammates [[Jansin Turgut]] and [[Callum Lancaster]]. He made 20 appearances in the 2015 season and scored a total of four tries. Abdull was given the number 13 shirt after [[Joe Westerman]] left Hull F.C. to join the [[Warrington Wolves]].
===Hull KR (loan)===
He spent with 2017 season on loan with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Kingstone Press Championship]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/latest-jordan-abdulls-return-hull-550378|title=Latest on Jordan Abdull's return to Hull FC and Hull KR injury news|work=Hull Daily Mail|date=29 September 2017|accessdate=17 January 2018}}</ref>
===London Broncos===
Abdull joined the [[London Broncos]] for an undisclosed fee ahead of the [[2019 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hullfc.com/news/2018-11-02-abdull-joins-broncos|title=Abdull Joins Broncos|publisher=Hull FC|date=2 November 2018|accessdate=2 January 2021}}</ref>

===Hull KR===
In round 3 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he kicked the winning field goal as Hull Kingston Rovers beat Huddersfield 25–24.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56734758|title=Hull Kingston Rovers 25–24 Huddersfield Giants – Hull KR score seven-second try|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 24 July 2021, Abdull scored two tries and kicked five goals in Hull KR's 32–30 defeat against [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57943411|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 32–30 Hull KR – Arthur Mourgue slots late kick as leaders win|work=BBC Sport|access-date=29 July 2021}}</ref>
On 24 September 2021, the day after being shortlisted for the Super League Man of Steel award, Abdull scored a try, two conversions, a penalty, and a drop goal, being named the Man of the Match, in Hull KR's shock 19–0 away win against Warrington, to earn a place in the Super League Play-Off semi-final at [[Catalans Dragons]].  Abdull played a total of 13 games for Hull KR in the [[2021 Super League season]] including the club's 28–10 semi-final loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com|accessdate=1 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com|accessdate=1 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0–19 Hull KR – Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=1 October 2021}}</ref>
On 20 October 2021 Abdull was selected for his [[England national rugby league team|England]] debut against [[France national rugby league team|France]]. On 12 May 2022, Abdull was ruled out for the remainder of the 2022 Super League season with a ligament tear.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jordan-abdull-injury-hull-kr-7072078/|title=Jordan Abdull's season over after Hull KR learn of injury severity|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|accessdate=26 October 2022}}</ref>
After a good start to the 2023 Super League season, it was announced on 4 May 2023 that Abdull had been ruled out for an indefinite period after tearing his quad muscle.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jordan-abdull-injury-hull-kr-8409501/|title=Hull KR rocked with Jordan Abdull handed brutal injury setback|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|accessdate=11 May 2023}}</ref>
Abdull played 14 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport|access-date=12 October 2023}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons (loan)===
In January 2024, it was announced that Abdull would join [[Catalans Dragons]] on a season-long loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://hullkr.co.uk/abdull-joins-catalans-dragons-on-season-long-loan/ |title=Abdull joins Catalans Dragons on season-long loan |website=Hull KR |date=5 January 2024 |access-date=5 January 2024}}</ref>
In June 2024, Abdull signed a three-year deal to join [[Hull F.C.]] ahead of the 2025 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/how-hull-fc-shaping-up-9325769/|title=How Hull FC are shaping up for 2025 season with eleven signings in new look side|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|accessdate=11 June 2024}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
On 24 September 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Widnes Vikings|date=24 Sep 2025|accessdate=28 Sep 2025|title=VIKINGS SIGN JORDAN ABDULL|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/vikings-sign-abdull/}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 17 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 1 month loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Apr 2026|accessdate=19 Apr 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Jordan Abdull set for swift Widnes Vikings exit as next move revealed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jordan-abdull-set-for-swift-widnes-vikings-exit#:~:text=Jordan%20Abdull%20is%20set%20to,next%20club%2C%20LoveRugbyLeague%20can%20reveal.}}</ref>

On 6 May 2026 it was reported that [[Sheffield Eagles]] had made the loan permanent and he had now joined on a 2½ year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 May 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Jordan Abdull makes permanent Widnes exit and joins new Championship club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jordan-abdull-makes-permanent-widnes-exit-and-joins-new-championship-club|accessdate=6 May 2026}}</ref>
